title: Header files should avoid containing unnamed namespaces
description: Header files should not contain unnamed namespacesAnalyze your codeintentionality-clearmaintainabilityCode Smellcertmisra-c++2008pitfallWhy is this an issue?More InfoAn unnamed namespace will be unique within each translation unit. Any declarations appearing in an unnamed namespace in a header will refer to a different entity in each translation unit, which is probably not the expected behavior.Noncompliant code example// Header.hpp namespace // Noncompliant { extern int32_t x; }// File1.cpp #include "Header.hpp" namespace { int32_t x; } void fn_a(void) { x = 42; }// File2.cpp #include "Header.hpp" namespace { int32_t x; // this is a different x than in File1.cpp } void fn_b(void) { fn_a(); // Is expected to initialize "x" to 42 if (x == 42) // But does not, as there are 2 distinct "x" var
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Header file contains unnamed namespace which should be avoided
